full_context: Application factor formula for bevel gear load capacity calculation in accordance with ISO 10300-1:2024 Chapter 5. Used to account for the influence of operating conditions on gear load.

variable_definitions.name: Application factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Factor considering the influence of operating conditions

variable_definitions.name: Nominal torque
variable_definitions.meaning: Actual torque transmitted by the gear

variable_definitions.name: Reference torque
variable_definitions.meaning: Design reference torque for the gear

potential_questions: A bevel gear has nominal torque T_nom=500 N·m and reference torque T_ref=400 N·m; find the application factor K_A.
task_description: 1. Obtain T_nom and T_ref. 2. Calculate K_A = T_nom / T_ref. 3. Output K_A.

full_context: Axial pitch formula for worm drives in accordance with ISO 10828:2024 Chapter 6. Used for geometric design of worm gear meshing.

variable_definitions.name: Axial pitch
variable_definitions.meaning: Distance between corresponding points of adjacent teeth along worm axis

variable_definitions.name: Normal module
variable_definitions.meaning: Module in the normal section of the worm

variable_definitions.name: Helix angle
variable_definitions.meaning: Angle between worm helix and axis

potential_questions: A worm has normal module m_n=4 mm and helix angle β=20°; find the axial pitch ρ_a.
task_description: 1. Obtain m_n and β. 2. Calculate cosβ. 3. Calculate ρ_a = m_n / cosβ. 4. Output ρ_a.

full_context: Local contact stress formula for crane wheel-rail contact in accordance with ISO 16881-1:2024 Chapter 7. Used for contact strength verification of crane wheels.

variable_definitions.name: Local contact stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Compressive stress in the wheel-rail contact zone

variable_definitions.name: Wheel load
variable_definitions.meaning: Vertical load transmitted by one wheel

variable_definitions.name: Contact width
variable_definitions.meaning: Contact width between wheel and rail

variable_definitions.name: Contact length
variable_definitions.meaning: Contact length between wheel and rail

potential_questions: Crane wheel load P=50 kN, contact width b=50 mm, contact length l=100 mm; find local contact stress σ_loc.
task_description: 1. Obtain P, b, l. 2. Calculate b·l. 3. Calculate σ_loc = P/(b·l). 4. Output σ_loc.

full_context: CO2 emission intensity formula for electric arc furnace steel plants in accordance with ISO 14404-2:2024 Chapter 4. Used to evaluate carbon efficiency of steel production.

variable_definitions.name: CO2 emission intensity
variable_definitions.meaning: CO2 emission per ton of steel produced

variable_definitions.name: Total CO2 emission
variable_definitions.meaning: Total CO2 generated in steelmaking process

variable_definitions.name: Steel production
variable_definitions.meaning: Total output of steel product

potential_questions: Annual CO2 emission E_CO2=50000 t CO2, annual steel output P=100000 t; find CO2 emission intensity EI_CO2.
task_description: 1. Obtain E_CO2 and P. 2. Calculate EI_CO2 = E_CO2 / P. 3. Output EI_CO2.

full_context: Sheath eddy current loss factor formula for power cables in accordance with IEC 60287-1-2:2023 Chapter 5. Used for cable thermal design.

variable_definitions.name: Sheath eddy current loss factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient representing eddy current loss in cable sheath

variable_definitions.name: Sheath resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Unit length resistance of metallic sheath

variable_definitions.name: Conductor resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Unit length AC resistance of conductor

variable_definitions.name: Geometric parameter
variable_definitions.meaning: Parameter related to cable layout geometry

potential_questions: Sheath resistance R_s=0.0002 Ω/m, conductor resistance R=0.0001 Ω/m, geometric parameter X=0.5; find sheath eddy current loss factor λ₂.
task_description: 1. Obtain R_s, R, X. 2. Calculate R_s/R. 3. Calculate X². 4. Calculate 1+X². 5. Calculate X²/(1+X²). 6. Calculate λ₂ = (R_s/R) · (X²/(1+X²)). 7. Output λ₂.

full_context: Short-circuit peak current formula for three-phase AC systems in accordance with IEC 60909-2016 Chapter 6. Used for electrodynamic stability verification of equipment.

variable_definitions.name: Short-circuit peak current
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum instantaneous value of short-circuit current

variable_definitions.name: Peak factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Factor accounting for the DC component of short-circuit current

variable_definitions.name: Initial symmetrical short-circuit current
variable_definitions.meaning: Initial symmetrical short-circuit current

potential_questions: Initial symmetrical short-circuit current I_k''=10 kA, peak factor κ=1.8; find short-circuit peak current i_p.
task_description: 1. Obtain κ and I_k''. 2. Calculate √2 I_k''. 3. Calculate i_p = κ · √2 I_k''. 4. Output i_p.

full_context: Power factor formula for rotating electrical machines in accordance with IEC 60034-1:2017 Chapter 9. Used to evaluate motor power quality.

variable_definitions.name: Power factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of active power to apparent power

variable_definitions.name: Input active power
variable_definitions.meaning: Input active power of the motor

variable_definitions.name: Apparent power
variable_definitions.meaning: Product of rms voltage and rms current

potential_questions: Motor input active power P₁=90 kW, apparent power S=100 kVA; find power factor cosφ.
task_description: 1. Obtain P₁ and S. 2. Calculate cosφ = P₁ / S. 3. Output cosφ.

full_context: Safety integrity level definition formula in accordance with IEC 61508:2010 Chapter 8. Used for safety instrumented system classification.

variable_definitions.name: Safety integrity level
variable_definitions.meaning: Reliability level of safety-related systems performing safety functions

variable_definitions.name: Average probability of failure on demand
variable_definitions.meaning: Average probability of failure on demand

potential_questions: A safety instrumented system has PFD_avg=0.001; find the safety integrity level SIL.
task_description: 1. Obtain PFD_avg. 2. Calculate -log₁₀(PFD_avg). 3. Output SIL.

full_context: Lightning risk assessment formula in accordance with IEC 62305:2010 Chapter 4. Used for lightning protection risk assessment of buildings.

variable_definitions.name: Lightning risk
variable_definitions.meaning: Risk value combining lightning frequency, damage probability and consequences

variable_definitions.name: Expected annual number of lightning strikes
variable_definitions.meaning: Expected annual lightning strikes on a building

variable_definitions.name: Damage probability
variable_definitions.meaning: Probability of lightning causing damage

variable_definitions.name: Loss value
variable_definitions.meaning: Economic loss or casualty value caused by damage

potential_questions: A building has expected annual strikes N_P=0.5 per year, damage probability P=0.1, loss L=100 ten thousand yuan; find lightning risk R.
task_description: 1. Obtain N_P, P, L. 2. Calculate R = N_P × P × L. 3. Output R.

full_context: Flame propagation value formula for elastomeric gaskets in accordance with ASTM C1166:2022 Chapter 5. Used to evaluate flame retardant performance.

variable_definitions.name: Flame propagation value
variable_definitions.meaning: Quantitative index of material flame propagation behavior

variable_definitions.name: Initial reading
variable_definitions.meaning: Measured reading at the start of test

variable_definitions.name: Final reading
variable_definitions.meaning: Measured reading at the end of test

potential_questions: Six specimens have initial readings R_io: 100,105,98,102,107,103 and final readings R_if:80,85,78,82,87,83; find flame propagation value FP.
task_description: 1. Obtain all R_io and R_if. 2. Calculate each (R_io-R_if). 3. Sum Σ(R_io-R_if). 4. Calculate FP = sum / 6. 5. Output FP.

full_context: Flame spread index formula for building materials in accordance with ASTM E84:2023 Chapter 8. Used for fire classification of building materials.

variable_definitions.name: Flame spread index
variable_definitions.meaning: Quantitative index of surface flame spread ability

variable_definitions.name: Total area under curve
variable_definitions.meaning: Area enclosed by flame spread curve and time axis

potential_questions: Test area under curve A_T=25 m·min; find flame spread index FSI.
task_description: 1. Obtain A_T. 2. Judge if A_T ≤ 29.7. 3. Calculate FSI = 0.515 × A_T. 4. Output FSI.

full_context: Actuation strain formula for shape memory alloys in accordance with ASTM E3097:2023 Chapter 6. Used to evaluate SMA thermomechanical performance.

variable_definitions.name: Actuation strain
variable_definitions.meaning: Recoverable strain of SMA during thermal cycling

variable_definitions.name: Strain at low transition temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Strain measured at low transition temperature

variable_definitions.name: Strain at upper transition temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Strain measured at high transition temperature

potential_questions: SMA low temperature strain ε_LCT=0.03, high temperature strain ε_UCT=0.01; find actuation strain ε_act.
task_description: 1. Obtain ε_LCT and ε_UCT. 2. Calculate ε_act = ε_LCT - ε_UCT. 3. Output ε_act.

full_context: Compressive strength formula for dimension stone in accordance with ASTM C170/C170M-2024 Chapter 7. Used for mechanical property testing of stone.

variable_definitions.name: Compressive strength
variable_definitions.meaning: Material resistance to failure under compressive load

variable_definitions.name: Failure load
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum load at specimen failure

variable_definitions.name: Bearing area
variable_definitions.meaning: Effective load-bearing area of specimen

potential_questions: Stone specimen failure load W=50000 N, bearing area A=2500 mm²; find compressive strength C.
task_description: 1. Obtain W and A. 2. Calculate C = W / A. 3. Output C.

full_context: Water absorption formula for dimension stone in accordance with ASTM C97:2024 Chapter 6. Used to evaluate water absorption of stone.

variable_definitions.name: Water absorption
variable_definitions.meaning: Ability of material to absorb water

variable_definitions.name: Dry mass
variable_definitions.meaning: Mass of specimen in dry condition

variable_definitions.name: Saturated mass
variable_definitions.meaning: Mass of specimen after soaking

potential_questions: Dry mass A=500 g, soaked mass B=510 g; find water absorption WA.
task_description: 1. Obtain A and B. 2. Calculate B-A. 3. Calculate (B-A)/A. 4. Calculate WA = ((B-A)/A) × 100%. 5. Output WA.

full_context: Tensile strength formula for polymer matrix composites in accordance with ASTM D3039-2023 Chapter 5. Used for mechanical property testing of composites.

variable_definitions.name: Tensile strength
variable_definitions.meaning: Material resistance to failure under tensile load

variable_definitions.name: Maximum load
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum load recorded in tensile test

variable_definitions.name: Cross-sectional area
variable_definitions.meaning: Original cross-sectional area of test section

potential_questions: Composite specimen maximum load P_max=10000 N, cross-section area A=100 mm²; find tensile strength σ_t.
task_description: 1. Obtain P_max and A. 2. Calculate σ_t = P_max / A. 3. Output σ_t.

full_context: Design value of action effect formula in accordance with EN 1990:2002 Chapter 6. Used for structural limit state design.

variable_definitions.name: Design action effect
variable_definitions.meaning: Design value of action effect with safety factors

variable_definitions.name: Partial factor for permanent actions
variable_definitions.meaning: Safety factor for permanent actions

variable_definitions.name: Characteristic permanent action
variable_definitions.meaning: Representative value of permanent action

variable_definitions.name: Partial factor for variable actions
variable_definitions.meaning: Safety factor for variable actions

variable_definitions.name: Characteristic variable action
variable_definitions.meaning: Representative value of variable action

potential_questions: Permanent action G_k=50 kN, γ_G=1.35; variable action Q_k=30 kN, γ_Q=1.5; find design action effect E_d.
task_description: 1. Obtain γ_G, G_k, γ_Q, Q_k. 2. Calculate γ_G × G_k. 3. Calculate γ_Q × Q_k. 4. Calculate E_d = γ_G G_k + γ_Q Q_k. 5. Output E_d.

full_context: Minimum tensile reinforcement area formula for concrete structures in accordance with EN 1992-1-1:2004 Chapter 6. Used to ensure minimum reinforcement ratio.

variable_definitions.name: Minimum tensile reinforcement area
variable_definitions.meaning: Minimum tensile steel area to prevent brittle failure

variable_definitions.name: Mean tensile strength of concrete
variable_definitions.meaning: Mean axial tensile strength of concrete

variable_definitions.name: Characteristic yield strength of reinforcement
variable_definitions.meaning: Characteristic yield strength of steel bar

variable_definitions.name: Width of tension zone
variable_definitions.meaning: Width of concrete tension zone

variable_definitions.name: Effective depth
variable_definitions.meaning: Distance from compression edge to centroid of tension steel

potential_questions: C30 concrete f_ctm=2.9 MPa, steel f_yk=400 MPa, b_t=300 mm, d=500 mm; find A_s,min.
task_description: 1. Obtain f_ctm, f_yk, b_t, d. 2. Calculate f_ctm/f_yk. 3. Calculate 0.26 × (f_ctm/f_yk) × b_t × d. 4. Output A_s,min.

full_context: Plastic moment resistance formula for steel structures in accordance with EN 1993-1-1:2005 Chapter 5. Used for plastic design of steel beams.

variable_definitions.name: Design plastic moment resistance
variable_definitions.meaning: Moment resistance at full plastic cross-section

variable_definitions.name: Plastic section modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Sum of area moments about plastic neutral axis

variable_definitions.name: Yield strength
variable_definitions.meaning: Yield strength of structural steel

potential_questions: H-section plastic modulus W_pl=500000 mm³, yield strength f_y=235 MPa; find design moment resistance M_c,Rd.
task_description: 1. Obtain W_pl and f_y. 2. Calculate M_c,Rd = W_pl × f_y. 3. Output M_c,Rd.

full_context: Base shear formula for seismic design in accordance with EN 1998-1:2004 Chapter 4. Used for seismic design of buildings.

variable_definitions.name: Base shear
variable_definitions.meaning: Total horizontal shear at structure base under earthquake

variable_definitions.name: Design spectral acceleration
variable_definitions.meaning: Design acceleration corresponding to fundamental period

variable_definitions.name: Total structural mass
variable_definitions.meaning: Equivalent total mass of structure

variable_definitions.name: Correction factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Correction factor for higher mode effects

potential_questions: Design spectral acceleration S_d=1.96 m/s², mass m=100000 kg, factor λ=0.85; find base shear F_b.
task_description: 1. Obtain S_d, m, λ. 2. Calculate F_b = S_d × m × λ. 3. Output F_b.

full_context: Risk assessment formula for machinery safety in accordance with ISO 12100:2010 Chapter 5. Used for quantitative risk assessment of machinery.

variable_definitions.name: Risk value
variable_definitions.meaning: Quantified risk combining severity, probability and exposure

variable_definitions.name: Severity of harm
variable_definitions.meaning: Severity level of potential injury

variable_definitions.name: Probability of occurrence
variable_definitions.meaning: Likelihood level of harm event

variable_definitions.name: Exposure frequency
variable_definitions.meaning: Frequency level of personnel exposure to hazard

potential_questions: Severity S=4, probability P=3, exposure E=2 (1–5 scale); find risk value R.
task_description: 1. Obtain S, P, E. 2. Calculate R = S × P × E. 3. Output R.
